title: Identify the Known Variables
text: Start by identifying the known variables in the problem. What is the initial velocity, final velocity, and distance traveled?

title: Choose the Right Equation
text: Which kinematic equation relates initial velocity, final velocity, acceleration, and distance?

title: Apply the Kinematic Equation
text: Use the equation $$v^2$$ $$=$$ $$u^2$$ + 2as to find the acceleration. Here, v is the final velocity, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the distance.

title: Calculate the Acceleration
text: Substitute the known values into the equation to find the acceleration. v $$=$$ $$65.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, u $$=$$ $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, s $$=$$ $$0.250$$ $$m$$.

title: Find the Time of Acceleration
text: Now that you have the acceleration, use another kinematic equation to find the time. Which equation relates velocity, acceleration, and time?

title: Use the Equation for Time
text: Use the equation v $$=$$ u + at to solve for time $$t$$.

title: Calculate the Time
text: Substitute the known values into the equation to find the time. v $$=$$ $$65.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, u $$=$$ $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, a $$=$$ $$8450$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.
